content: I've finally put up the PR to migrate my old kernel module to Rust for Linux! it is so much cleaner and easier to read: https://github.com/jbaublitz/knock-out/pull/13 what you all have done so far is really cool!
I'm going to update the README to point to Rust-for-Linux
Thanks for providing the feedback on kernel module! I was doing a little bit of a speed run on the migration and it looks like I left some traces of the process in the final code :face_palm:
I have also written a few suggestions -- thanks a lot for migrating it!
Any suggestions on whether clippy should be specified as a Makefile target in an out-of-tree build even though it is an environment variable in the in-tree build?
Using the in-tree way should work, e.g. make KDIR=../linux LLVM=1 CLIPPY=1. Does it work for you?
i.e. I wouldn't create a target; that way it is consistent with in-tree so that people don't need to learn 2 different ways
Yes, I figured that might be your preference and that works for me! I can just add it into the git hooks that way.
